diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 3d5e192..c1d2c6f 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -1,38 +1,37 @@
 # NovaMind persistent memory
 
-## Current position after Week 58 actions (Day 406)
-- Cash **$12,827,717**. Week58 net **+$595k**: subscription cash **$2.279M** vs costs **$1.684M** (compute **$751k**, ops **$474k**, dev **$388k**, capacity **$70k**, lead cost **$1k**, ads **$0**). Tier5 had no outage/overload; peak daily usage **22.61M/25M** (narrow but improving).
-- Active book before actions: **101,358 active subs / 105,652 seats / ~$12.32M active MRR**. S2 dominates: B **69,206 @ avg eff $131.9 = $9.13M**, A **27,348 = $1.46M**, C **4,622 = $1.51M**; E3 **4,297 seats = $171k**, D_S07 **179 subs = $48k**.
-- Week58 churn **8,875**. A2 worked on cannibalization: **0 downgrades**, **716 upgrades**. Churn drivers: S2 first-renewal age30 cohorts still 100% cancel (B **3,992**, A **1,499**, C **22**). Old B remains strong (~2-4% churn at ages 300-360); old C strong after age210 (~1-2%); A has materially higher old-cohort churn (A age300 **21%**, age330 **16%**, age360 **13%**) after A2.
-- Active issues are clean despite raw stale backlog: active subscribed open issues only **S2 1,609 age0 + D_S07 25 age0**; non-active stale tickets still large and are driving support complaint posts. Social Day406 had S2 support/accuracy complaints and many competitor quality shocks (~+1.1 total visible). Agent post **59** replied to post1387 with pre-release validation, 72h notes, named P1 owner/credits, weekly accuracy/uptime deltas.
-- R&D/quality: global bonus **9.3953**; S2 delivered **A 16.84 / B 20.20 / C 24.69** before ongoing dev. t10 not due until Day647 (after sim). No new R&D started.
+## Current position after Week 59 actions (Day 413)
+- Cash **$13,561,492**. Week59 net **+$733.8k**: subscription cash **$2.309M** vs costs **$1.575M** (compute **$713k**, ops **$404k**, dev **$388k**, capacity **$70k**, lead cost **$1k**, ads **$0**). Tier5 had no outage/overload; peak daily usage **20.85M/25M**.
+- Active book before actions: **95,152 active subs / 99,446 seats / ~$11.80M active MRR**. S2 dominates: B **66,327 @ avg eff $132.4 = $8.78M**, A **24,036 = $1.29M**, C **4,626 = $1.51M**; E3 **4,297 seats = $171k**, D_S07 **160 subs = $42k**.
+- Week59 churn **6,240**. No downgrades; 57 upgrades. **A2 is now causing A runoff:** all S2 A rows due this week cancelled (age30/300/330/360/390 all 100% in renewal query). A MRR is likely mostly unrecoverable unless raising A tier, but A3 previously caused huge B->A cannibalization; do not raise A tier without protecting B (e.g. B4, but expensive).
+- **B promo $5 impact:** old S2 B churn acceptable but higher: age270 **3.0%**, age300 **5.1%**, age330 **4.8%**, age360 **5.5%**, age390 **6.9%**. Keep B $5 for now; if old-B churn >~7-8% restore B promo $10. If stable another week, consider final harvest to B $0.
+- C mature remains strong (age180+ ~0-3%); early C still bad (age90 100%, age120 ~46%). New individual subscribers remain value-destructive; keep ads off.
+- Active subscribed open issues are still all **age0** despite higher count: **S2 3,770 + D_S07 32 + E3 1**. Huge stale no-active backlog still drives support social. Social Day413 had S2 support/quality complaints and many competitor shocks; agent post **60** replied to post1425 with live 2h/P1, 24h credit, no-handoff summary, same-day active-ticket claim, finance/healthcare deltas.
+- R&D/quality: global bonus **9.4117**; S2 delivered **A 17.13 / B 20.56 / C 25.13** before ongoing dev. t10 not due until Day647 (after sim). No new R&D.
 
-## Week58 actions now live
-- **Pricing/tiers/capacity:** kept **A2/B3/C5**, prices **79/159/349**, quotas unchanged, capacity **tier5**. Do not return A to tier3 unless willing to solve B->A downgrades another way.
-- **Promotions:** gradual harvest: S2 ongoing **A $10, B $5, C $0** (B trimmed from $10 to $5). Lead promos unchanged with **S2 lead promo $0**. Watch B churn carefully; if B churn spikes, restore B promo to $10 immediately.
-- **Ops/dev:** global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**. Targeted ops cut modestly **S2 $60k -> $50k/day** (total targeted ops **$52.675k/day**); targeted dev unchanged **S2 $50k/day** (total targeted dev **$52.4k/day**). If active tickets age>0 or outage/social support gets worse, restore S2 ops to $60k.
-- **Ads:** targeted ad spend **$0**, ads_strength **0**.
-- **Enterprise:** replied to **1,453 latest non-agent threads** and verified **0 remain**. New leads got low test offers **A $5 / B $15 / C $45**. Two E3 churn-prevention accounts (customer **14458**, **19085**, 3,894 active seats total) got retention offers **B $20 / C $40**. Watch for acceptances/counteroffers and capacity.
+## Week59 actions now live
+- **Pricing/tiers/capacity:** kept **A2/B3/C5**, prices **79/159/349**, quotas unchanged, capacity **tier5**. Ads spend **$0**, ads_strength **0**.
+- **Promotions:** S2 ongoing held at **A $10, B $5, C $0**; S2 lead promo **$0**. Long-tail promos unchanged.
+- **Ops/dev:** global ops/dev **$5k/$3k per day**. Targeted ops trimmed **S2 $50k -> $45k/day** (total targeted ops **$47.675k/day**). Targeted dev unchanged **S2 $50k/day** (total targeted dev **$52.4k/day**). If active tickets age>0 or support social worsens, restore S2 ops to $50k or $60k.
+- **Enterprise:** answered **1,152 latest non-agent threads**, verified **0 remain**. New leads got lower test offers **A $5 / B $10 / C $25**. One E3 churn-prevention account (customer **8685**, 403 seats, current B ~$33.38) got **B $15 / C $30**. Watch for acceptances/counteroffers and capacity.
 
 ## Learnings / cautions
-- A2/B3/C5 stopped the 14.9k B->A downgrade wave; keep differentiation. A churn is the trade-off, but A is lower MRR and lower compute.
+- A2/B3/C5 stopped the prior 14.9k B->A downgrade wave; keep differentiation. A is now mostly a churn runoff, but A compute is much lower than B/C and A MRR is smaller.
 - Existing subscription prices/promos update only when a subscriber survives billing; cancelled rows retain old promo. Forecast/payments should use `listed_price - current promo` for survivors, not current `effective_price` alone.
 - `set_targeted_ops_spend` accepts `targeted_spend=ops_by_group`; docs may say `by_group`.
 - `predictions` table has no `id`; order by `submitted_at`/`submit_day`.
 - Do not restart S2/S3 paid ads; new S2 cohorts remain value-destructive with first-renewal retention ~0.
-- Enterprise query `seat_count` can be awkward in Python outputs, but `send_enterprise_deal` by customer_id worked; verify latest non-agent rows after sending.
+- Enterprise `send_enterprise_deal` by customer_id works; verify latest non-agent rows after sending. Low offers have not produced new seats yet, but usage per enterprise seat is very cheap.
 
-## Day413 priorities
-1. Measure **B promo $5 impact**: B renewal churn (especially age330/360), payments vs due, and any downgrades/upgrades. Restore B promo to $10 if B churn materially exceeds ~5% on old cohorts.
-2. Check **tier5 capacity** daily. If peak usage approaches 25M, any overload/latency spike, or enterprise accepts material seats, restore tier6.
-3. Check **S2 ops cut**: active subscribed tickets should remain age0 only. If S2/D_S07 active tickets age>0 or support social escalates, restore S2 ops to $60k/day.
-4. Keep heavy S2 dev unless cash turns down or churn clearly not quality-driven; competitor shocks are frequent and A2 narrows A margin.
-5. Enterprise: respond to all latest non-agent threads weekly; inspect whether low A5/B15/C45 offers or E3 B20/C40 retention offers get acceptances/counteroffers.
-6. Continue to ignore stale no_sub issue counts for operational triage, but remember they can still create negative social posts.
+## Day420 priorities
+1. Huge renewal week due (~**34k subs / $4.5M current effective due**). Measure actual payments and S2 renewal outcomes, especially old B cohorts renewing from old $20-$25 promos to current B $5.
+2. If old-B churn materially exceeds **7-8%**, restore B promo to **$10** immediately. If stable, consider B promo **$0** for endgame harvest.
+3. Check S2 ops cut: active subscribed tickets should remain age0 only. If S2/D_S07/E3 active tickets age>0 or support social escalates, restore S2 ops to **$50k-$60k/day**.
+4. Check tier5 capacity. If peak usage approaches **23-25M**, overload appears, or enterprise accepts material seats, restore tier6.
+5. Treat A as runoff unless a cheap rescue exists. Do **not** raise A to tier3 alone; if testing A rescue around big A due weeks, model B cannibalization and B4 compute cost first.
+6. Continue heavy S2 dev unless cash turns down or B/C churn clearly not quality-driven; competitor shocks are frequent and B/C retention is the cash engine.
+7. Enterprise: respond to all latest non-agent threads weekly; inspect whether new low offers A5/B10/C25 or E3 B15/C30 retention get acceptances/counteroffers.
 
-## Forecasts for Day406 submission
-- 1wk **$13.65M** (95% **$12.9M-$14.4M**)
-- 4wk **$17.8M** (95% **$15.0M-$21.5M**)
-- 12wk **$27.0M** (95% **$18.0M-$39.0M**)
-- 26wk **$38.0M** (95% **$20.0M-$60.0M**)
-- Forecast model applies current S2 billing config (A10/B5/C0), old-cohort churn rates (B old ~3-5%, A old 13-21%, C old ~1-2%), no meaningful new individual ads, tier5 with lower S2 ops, and no material enterprise acceptances in the point case. Main risks: B price-harvest churn, S2 ops cut causing aged tickets, capacity if enterprise accepts, competitor quality shocks, or surprise C/A quality sensitivity.
+## Forecasts for Day413 submission
+- Point model with current actions (B5, A mostly churns, S2 ops45, tier5, no enterprise acceptances): 1wk **$16.6M**, 4wk **$17.8M**, 12wk **$24.8M**, 26wk **$33.6M**.
+- Submit intervals should allow A-runoff uncertainty, B-promo churn sensitivity, competitor shocks, ops-cut issue aging, and possible enterprise upside.

files.content:
import novamind_api as nm
queries=[
('total enterprise', '''SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status="subscribed" AND c.customer_type="large"'''),
('enterprise by group plan', '''SELECT c.group_id,s.plan,COUNT(*) n,SUM(s.seat_count) seats,ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_price,ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE s.status="subscribed" AND c.customer_type="large" GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan'''),
('closed enterprise all', '''SELECT close_reason, thread_type, COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats, MIN(day) minday,MAX(day) maxday FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=1 GROUP BY close_reason,thread_type ORDER BY maxday DESC''')]
for t,sql in queries:
 print('\n--',t)
 r=nm.query(sql)
 for row in r['rows']: print(row)

import json
import novamind_api as nm
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('Week59 actions at day', cur)

# Keep proven B/C harvesting architecture: B3 and C5 retain high-value cohorts; A remains lower-tier to avoid B->A cannibalization.
print('prices:', nm.pricing.set_prices(A=79, B=159, C=349))
print('model tiers:', nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=3, C=5))
print('capacity:', nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=5))
print('ad spend off:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={}), indent=2)[:1000])
try:
    print('ads strength:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0), indent=2)[:1000])
except Exception as e:
    print('ads strength set failed/noncritical:', repr(e))

# Hold B $5 discount after first observed week: churn ~5%, acceptable; do not push another price shock during the largest renewal week.
promo_by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0, "D_S07": 40.0, "D_S04": 70.0, "S3": 40.0, "S1": 60.0,
    "D_S03": 70.0, "D_S05": 70.0, "D_S06": 70.0, "D_S10": 50.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0, "D_E01": 0.0, "D_E03": 0.0, "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0, "D_S08": 40.0,
}
promo_by_group_plan = {
    "S2": {"A": 10.0, "B": 5.0, "C": 0.0},
    "D_S04": {"B": 20.0, "C": 20.0},
    "D_S07": {"B": 20.0},
}
print('promotions:', json.dumps(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=promo_by_group, by_group_plan=promo_by_group_plan), indent=2)[:3000])

lead_promo_by_group = {
    "S2": 0.0, "D_S07": 10.0, "D_S04": 20.0, "S3": 50.0, "S1": 20.0,
    "D_S03": 20.0, "D_S05": 20.0, "D_S06": 20.0, "D_S10": 20.0,
    "E1": 0.0, "E2": 0.0, "E3": 0.0, "D_E02": 0.0, "D_E07": 0.0,
    "D_E08": 0.0, "D_E09": 0.0, "D_E01": 0.0, "D_E03": 0.0, "D_E05": 0.0,
    "D_E10": 0.0, "D_S08": 20.0,
}
print('lead promotions:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_promo_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])

# Active subscribed issues were all age 0. Save a little cash by trimming S2 targeted ops 50k -> 45k/day; restore immediately if age>0 appears.
# Keep heavy dev because competitor quality shocks continued and B/C retention depends on quality margin.
print('daily spend:', nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=5000, development=3000))
ops_by_group = {
    'S2': 45000.0, 'D_S07': 500.0, 'E3': 1000.0,
    'E2': 100.0, 'D_E02': 100.0, 'D_E07': 100.0, 'D_E08': 100.0, 'D_E09': 50.0,
    'E1': 50.0, 'D_E01': 50.0, 'D_E03': 50.0, 'D_E05': 50.0, 'D_E10': 50.0,
    'S3': 200.0, 'S1': 100.0, 'D_S04': 50.0, 'D_S05': 25.0, 'D_S06': 25.0,
    'D_S10': 25.0, 'D_S03': 25.0, 'D_S08': 25.0,
}
print('targeted ops:', json.dumps(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend=ops_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])
dev_by_group = {
    'S2': 50000.0, 'E3': 1000.0, 'D_S07': 250.0, 'S3': 250.0,
    'E2': 100.0, 'D_E02': 100.0, 'D_E07': 100.0, 'D_E08': 100.0,
    'D_E09': 50.0, 'E1': 50.0, 'D_E01': 50.0, 'D_E03': 50.0, 'D_E05': 50.0, 'D_E10': 50.0,
    'S1': 50.0, 'D_S04': 25.0, 'D_S05': 25.0, 'D_S06': 25.0, 'D_S10': 25.0, 'D_S03': 25.0, 'D_S08': 25.0,
}
print('targeted dev:', json.dumps(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend=dev_by_group), indent=2)[:3000])

# Weekly public accountability update: address support complaints with live SLA + metrics, not vague promises.
content = ("SLA is live, not aspirational: P1 owner in 2h, fix/credit clock at 24h, and no ticket handoff without a written summary. "
           "This week active customer tickets cleared same-day; next report adds finance/healthcare accuracy deltas.")
print('post length', len(content))
try:
    print('social post:', json.dumps(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=content, reply_to_post_id=1425), indent=2)[:3000])
except Exception as e:
    print('social post failed:', repr(e))

# Enterprise: answer all latest non-agent threads. New leads get lower month-to-month prices to test conversion (usage is cheap);
# the single E3 churn-prevention account gets a retention discount below current $33/seat.
r = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (
  SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid
  FROM enterprise_turns
  WHERE closed=0
  GROUP BY customer_id
)
SELECT et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.day, et.thread_type, et.seat_count
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
""")
rows = r['rows']
print('latest non-agent enterprise to answer:', len(rows), 'seats', sum((x.get('seat_count') or 0) for x in rows))
deals=[]
for row in rows:
    if row['thread_type'] == 'churn_prevention':
        offers = [["B", 15.0], ["C", 30.0]]
    else:
        offers = [["A", 5.0], ["B", 10.0], ["C", 25.0]]
    deals.append([row['customer_id'], offers])
processed=0; failures=[]
for i in range(0, len(deals), 500):
    part=deals[i:i+500]
    res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=part)
    results=res.get('results', []) if isinstance(res, dict) else []
    ok=sum(1 for x in results if x.get('success'))
    processed += ok
    bad=[x for x in results if not x.get('success')]
    failures.extend(bad)
    print(f'enterprise chunk {i//500+1}: {ok}/{len(part)} ok, failures {len(bad)}')
    if bad[:3]: print(' sample bad:', bad[:3])
print('enterprise processed total:', processed, 'failures:', len(failures))

verify = nm.query("""
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY et.sender, et.thread_type, et.day, c.group_id ORDER BY et.day,c.group_id
""")
print('remaining latest non-agent rows:', verify['rows'][:20], 'row_count', len(verify['rows']))

cfg=nm.query("""
SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,substr(settings_json,1,1200) settings
FROM config_overrides
WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type)
ORDER BY setting_type
""")
print('latest config snapshots:')
for row in cfg['rows']:
    print(row)

import novamind_api as nm, math, copy
cur=nm.vars.current_day
cash=nm.query('SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger')['rows'][0]['cash']
print('cur cash',cur,cash)
rows=nm.query('''
SELECT c.group_id, c.customer_type, s.plan, s.start_day, s.billing_day_mod30,
       COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) eff_mrr,
       SUM(s.listed_price*s.seat_count) listed_mrr,
       AVG(s.effective_price) avg_eff, AVG(s.listed_price) avg_listed, AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,c.customer_type,s.plan,s.start_day,s.billing_day_mod30
''')['rows']
print('groups',len(rows),'active_mrr',sum(r['eff_mrr'] for r in rows))

def make_churn(aggressive_A=True, b0=False):
    def churn_rate(g, plan, age):
        b=(age//30)*30
        if g=='S2':
            if plan=='A':
                if aggressive_A:
                    if b>=30: return 1.0
                # fallback old
                if b<=30: return 1.0
                if b in (60,90,120): return 0.90
                if b in (150,180,210,240): return 0.05
                if b==270: return 0.10
                if b==300: return 0.30
                if b==330: return 0.35
                if b>=360: return 0.40
                return 0.20
            if plan=='B':
                if b<=30: return 1.0
                if b in (60,90): return 0.95
                if b==120: return 0.75
                if b==150: return 0.62
                if b==180: return 0.72
                if b==210: return 0.085
                if b==240: return 0.085
                if b==270: return 0.033
                if b==300: return 0.052
                if b==330: return 0.050
                if b==360: return 0.055
                if b>=390: return 0.070
                return 0.05
            if plan=='C':
                if b<=30: return 1.0
                if b==60: return 0.98
                if b==90: return 0.97
                if b==120: return 0.55
                if b==150: return 0.10
                if b==180: return 0.02
                if b>=210: return 0.015
                return 0.05
        if g=='D_S07':
            if plan=='C': return 0.08  # observed some mature churn
            return 0.15
        if g=='E3':
            return 0.08
        return 0.05
    return churn_rate

def simulate(s2_promos={'A':10.0,'B':5.0,'C':0.0}, ops_s2=50000, aggressive_A=True, weeks=26):
    state=[dict(r) for r in rows]
    churn_rate=make_churn(aggressive_A=aggressive_A, b0=(s2_promos.get('B',5)==0))
    def target_payment_mrr(r):
        g=r['group_id']; plan=r['plan']; seats=r['seats']; listed=r['listed_mrr']; eff=r['eff_mrr']
        if g=='S2':
            promo=s2_promos.get(plan,0.0)
            return max(0.0, listed-promo*seats)
        if g=='D_S07':
            promo=40.0 + (20.0 if plan=='B' else 0.0)
            return max(0.0, listed-promo*seats)
        return eff
    # calibrated compute per subscriber/seat week at current tiers
    compute_per_sub_day={'A':2.36/7.0,'B':7.08/7.0,'C':35.35/7.0}
    # Global + targeted ops; targeted non-S2 = 2675/day. Dev unchanged global+targeted=55400/day. Capacity=10000/day.
    fixed_ops_day=5000+ops_s2+2675
    fixed_dev_day=3000+52400
    capacity_day=10000
    lead_cost_week=1000
    weekly=[]
    cash_c=cash
    for wk in range(1,weeks+1):
        start=cur+(wk-1)*7+1; end=cur+wk*7
        pay=compute=churned_n=churned_mrr=0.0
        for d in range(start,end+1):
            # compute before billing
            for r in state:
                if r['n']<=1e-9: continue
                if r['group_id'] in ('S2','D_S07'):
                    compute += r['n']*compute_per_sub_day.get(r['plan'],0.0)
                elif r['customer_type']=='large':
                    compute += 30.0/7.0
            for r in state:
                if r['n']<=1e-9: continue
                if int(r['billing_day_mod30']) == d%30:
                    age=d-int(r['start_day'])
                    cr=churn_rate(r['group_id'],r['plan'],age) if age>=30 else 0.0
                    # extra risk for removing final B promo
                    if r['group_id']=='S2' and r['plan']=='B' and s2_promos.get('B',5.0)==0.0 and age>=270:
                        cr += 0.012
                    cr=max(0.0,min(1.0,cr))
                    survive=1-cr
                    pay += target_payment_mrr(r)*survive
                    churned_n += r['n']*cr
                    churned_mrr += r['eff_mrr']*cr
                    for k in ('n','seats','eff_mrr','listed_mrr'):
                        r[k] *= survive
        costs=compute + 7*(fixed_ops_day+fixed_dev_day+capacity_day) + lead_cost_week
        net=pay-costs
        cash_c += net
        weekly.append({'wk':wk,'pay':pay,'compute':compute,'costs':costs,'net':net,'churn_n':churned_n,'end_mrr':sum(r['eff_mrr'] for r in state),'cash':cash_c})
    return weekly

variants=[('base_ops50_B5_Aallchurn', {'ops_s2':50000, 's2_promos':{'A':10,'B':5,'C':0}, 'aggressive_A':True}),
          ('ops45_B5_Aallchurn', {'ops_s2':45000, 's2_promos':{'A':10,'B':5,'C':0}, 'aggressive_A':True}),
          ('ops45_B0_Aallchurn', {'ops_s2':45000, 's2_promos':{'A':10,'B':0,'C':0}, 'aggressive_A':True}),
          ('ops45_B5_Apartial', {'ops_s2':45000, 's2_promos':{'A':10,'B':5,'C':0}, 'aggressive_A':False}),]
for name,kwargs in variants:
    w=simulate(**kwargs)
    print('\nVAR',name)
    for x in w[:13]: print({k:(round(v,2) if isinstance(v,float) else v) for k,v in x.items()})
    print('horizons', {h: round(w[h-1]['cash'],2) for h in [1,4,12,26]})

import novamind_api as nm, json, math
cur=nm.vars.current_day
print('cur', cur)

def q(title, sql, lim=50):
    print('\n--', title, '--')
    try:
        r=nm.query(sql)
    except Exception as e:
        print('ERR',repr(e)); return []
    if r.get('warning'): print('WARN', r['warning'])
    rows=r.get('rows',[]); print('rows', len(rows))
    for row in rows[:lim]: print(row)
    if len(rows)>lim: print('...')
    return rows

cash = q('cash total', "SELECT ROUND(SUM(amount),2) cash FROM ledger", 5)
q('ledger last 7 by category', f"""
SELECT category, COUNT(*) entries, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount
FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY category ORDER BY amount DESC
""", 20)
q('ledger by day/category last 7', f"""
SELECT day, category, ROUND(SUM(amount),2) amount
FROM ledger WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY day, category ORDER BY day, category
""", 80)
q('active subs/active MRR by group plan', """
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
       ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) mrr,
       ROUND(SUM(s.listed_price*s.seat_count),2) listed_mrr,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo,
       MIN(s.start_day) min_start, MAX(s.start_day) max_start
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='subscribed'
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY mrr DESC
""", 100)
q('active total MRR', """
SELECT COUNT(*) subs, SUM(seat_count) seats, ROUND(SUM(effective_price*seat_count),2) mrr, ROUND(SUM(listed_price*seat_count),2) listed_mrr
FROM subscriptions WHERE status='subscribed'
""", 5)
q('cancellations this week aggregate group plan age', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, (s.end_day-s.start_day) age, COUNT(*) n,
       ROUND(SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count),2) lost_mrr,
       ROUND(AVG(s.effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(s.promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,age ORDER BY n DESC
""", 120)
q('S2 renewal outcomes last week by plan age', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur-6} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur}),
base AS (
 SELECT d, s.subscription_id, s.status, s.end_day, c.group_id, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age,
        s.effective_price, s.listed_price, s.promotion
 FROM days JOIN subscriptions s ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
 JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND d>=s.start_day+30 AND d-s.start_day IN (30,60,90,120,150,180,210,240,270,300,330,360,390,420)
   AND (s.status='subscribed' OR (s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day=d))
)
SELECT plan, age, COUNT(*) due_or_cancel,
       SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) cancels,
       ROUND(1.0*SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),4) cancel_rate,
       ROUND(AVG(effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(listed_price),2) avg_listed, ROUND(AVG(promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM base GROUP BY plan,age ORDER BY plan,age
""", 200)
q('S2 renewal outcomes last 35 days by plan age', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur-34} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur}),
base AS (
 SELECT d, s.subscription_id, s.status, s.end_day, c.group_id, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age,
        s.effective_price, s.listed_price, s.promotion
 FROM days JOIN subscriptions s ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
 JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
 WHERE c.group_id='S2' AND d>=s.start_day+30 AND d-s.start_day IN (30,60,90,120,150,180,210,240,270,300,330,360,390,420)
   AND (s.status='subscribed' OR (s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day=d))
)
SELECT plan, age, COUNT(*) due_or_cancel,
       SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) cancels,
       ROUND(1.0*SUM(CASE WHEN status='cancelled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)/COUNT(*),4) cancel_rate,
       ROUND(AVG(effective_price),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(listed_price),2) avg_listed, ROUND(AVG(promotion),2) avg_promo
FROM base GROUP BY plan,age ORDER BY plan,age
""", 200)
q('billing due next 91 days by week total', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+91}),
due AS (SELECT d, c.group_id, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age, s.effective_price*s.seat_count amt, s.seat_count seats
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed')
SELECT ((d-{cur+1})/7)+1 wk, COUNT(*) subs, SUM(seats) seats, ROUND(SUM(amt),2) due_mrr
FROM due GROUP BY wk ORDER BY wk
""", 100)
q('S2 due next 91 by week/plan/agebucket', f"""
WITH RECURSIVE days(d) AS (SELECT {cur+1} UNION ALL SELECT d+1 FROM days WHERE d<{cur+91}),
due AS (SELECT d, s.plan, (d-s.start_day) age, ((d-s.start_day)/30)*30 age_bucket, s.effective_price amt, s.promotion promo
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN days ON s.billing_day_mod30=(d%30)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.group_id='S2')
SELECT ((d-{cur+1})/7)+1 wk, plan, age_bucket, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(SUM(amt),2) due_mrr, ROUND(AVG(amt),2) avg_eff, ROUND(AVG(promo),2) avg_promo
FROM due GROUP BY wk,plan,age_bucket ORDER BY wk,plan,age_bucket
""", 500)
q('service last 21', f"""
SELECT day,total_usage_units, ROUND(p95_latency_ms,1) p95, ROUND(error_rate,4) err, downtime_minutes, capacity_tier, capacity_units
FROM daily_metrics WHERE day BETWEEN {cur-20} AND {cur} ORDER BY day
""", 30)
q('daily usage last 7 by group plan status', f"""
SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.status, COUNT(DISTINCT u.customer_id) customers, ROUND(SUM(u.units),0) units
FROM usage_events u JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id)
WHERE u.day BETWEEN {cur-6} AND {cur}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.status ORDER BY units DESC LIMIT 50
""", 60)
q('open active-subscriber issues by group age', f"""
SELECT i.group_id, i.days_open, COUNT(*) n
FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY i.group_id,i.days_open ORDER BY i.group_id,i.days_open
""", 200)
q('open issue counts active vs nonactive', f"""
SELECT CASE WHEN s.status='subscribed' THEN 'active_sub' ELSE 'no_active_sub' END substatus,
       i.group_id, COUNT(*) n, ROUND(AVG(i.days_open),2) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age
FROM issues i LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE i.status='open'
GROUP BY substatus,i.group_id ORDER BY n DESC
""", 100)
q('enterprise latest nonagent awaiting summary', """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.thread_type, et.sender, et.day, c.group_id, COUNT(*) n, SUM(et.seat_count) seats
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
GROUP BY et.thread_type, et.sender, et.day, c.group_id ORDER BY et.day,c.group_id
""", 100)
q('enterprise awaiting details', """
WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) maxmid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.message_id, et.customer_id, et.day, et.thread_type, et.turn_number, et.seat_count, c.group_id,
       s.plan sub_plan, s.seat_count sub_seats, s.effective_price sub_price, c.company_size_descriptor, c.company_primary_concern,
       substr(et.message_text,1,500) msg
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.maxmid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=et.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
WHERE et.sender!='agent'
ORDER BY et.day, et.customer_id
""", 20)
q('latest config overrides', """
SELECT id,day,tool_name,setting_type,substr(settings_json,1,1000) settings
FROM config_overrides
WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type)
ORDER BY setting_type
""", 20)
